items.title: Open coding agent models with benchmarks
items.oneliner: MIT-licensed coding models and scaffolds you can run today, with 9B to 397B variants and published benchmark results.

items.title: CUDA apps on non Nvidia GPUs
items.oneliner: Compatibility layer that runs unmodified CUDA workloads on AMD GPUs, including Blender and early PhysX support.

items.title: Linux boots on Sega MegaDrive
items.oneliner: Cloneable port that brings Linux to Sega MegaDrive hardware, with docs for experimenting on retro consoles.

items.title: CUDA kernel execution deep dive
items.oneliner: Hands-on walkthrough tracing a simple CUDA kernel from nvcc compilation to GPU launch and device memory traffic.

items.title: SQLite corruption failure mode guide
items.oneliner: Official guide showing exactly how SQLite files get corrupted, with concrete anti-patterns to avoid in production.

items.title: Safer TypeScript with parsing
items.oneliner: Practical TypeScript tutorial showing how parsing preserves type guarantees better than ad hoc validation.

items.title: Memory safe context switching in C
items.oneliner: Technical writeup on Fil-C's safe setjmp, longjmp, and ucontext support for coroutines without dangling stack execution.

items.title: Run PDP-1 Lisp today
items.oneliner: Beginner-friendly setup guide for exploring the 1960 PDP-1 Lisp system and its original interactive REPL.

items.title: Build an octocopter from scratch
items.oneliner: Detailed build log covering parts, wiring, and lessons learned while assembling a custom octocopter from zero experience.

items.title: Shader toolkit for layered effects
items.oneliner: Browser-based shader tool for stacking and animating effects quickly when you need visual prototypes without engine setup.
